description: Create or modify cable apply conditions. The following keywords and options are available:

6.0.description: Applies conditions to cable elements in the range. The following keywords and options are available:

keywords.name: f1
keywords.description: assign uniform applied distributed loads. Loads (force per unit length) are applied to all elements in the range. Positive loads act in the positive y- or z-directions (f1 and f2, respectively) of the element system and maintain this orientation during large-strain motion. Point loads are applied at the nodes using the command structure node apply.

keywords.name: tension
keywords.description: assign a specific axial tension value in the cable element that will be maintained during cycling. Incomplete sentence follows: This is intended as a way to pre-tension cables, but applying the pre-tension force as a tension to the end cable element(s), that are generally ungrouted. [DR: why is this flagged?] Note that this setting will override the normal force-displacement law for the cable element as long as it is active. Removing the condition will not remove the force, but from then on, the cable element forces will evolve naturally.

keywords.name: tension-active
keywords.description: make the tensile apply condition active or inactive. If active, the last specified value of tension is used, which is 0.0 by default.

keywords.name: tension-value
keywords.description: set the axial tensile force applied to the element f. This makes the condition active automatically.

7.0.description: Applies conditions to cable elements in the range. The following keywords and options are available:

keywords.name: adummy
keywords.description: assign uniform applied distributed loads. Loads (force per unit length) are applied to all elements in the range. Positive loads act in the positive \(y\)- or \(z\)-directions (f1 and f2, respectively) of the element system and maintain this orientation during large-strain motion. Point loads are applied at the nodes using the command structure node apply.

keywords.name: tension
keywords.description: assign a specific axial tension value in the cable element that will be maintained during cycling. Incomplete sentence follows: This is intended as a way to pre-tension cables, but applying the pre-tension force as a tension to the end cable element(s), that are generally ungrouted. [DR: why is this flagged?] Note that this setting will override the normal force-displacement law for the cable element as long as it is active. Removing the condition will not remove the force, but from then on, the cable element forces will evolve naturally.

keywords.name: tension-active
keywords.description: make the tensile apply condition active or inactive. If active, the last specified value of tension is used, which is 0.0 by default.

keywords.name: tension-value
keywords.description: set the axial tensile force applied to the element f. This makes the condition active automatically.

keywords.name: tension
keywords.description: assign a specific axial tension value in the cable element that will be maintained during cycling. This is intended as a way to pre-tension cables by applying the pre-tension force to the (generally ungrouted) end cable element(s). This setting will override the normal force-displacement law for the cable element as long as it is active. Removing the condition will not remove the force, but from then on, the cable element forces will evolve naturally. Two sub-keywords are available: active <b> makes the tensile apply condition active or inactive (if active, the last specified value of tension is used, default 0.0); value <f> sets the axial tensile force applied to the element, which makes the condition active automatically.
